annotations.label: Conjugate elements

anchor.snippet: of a group are conjugate if there is an element

annotations.note: `IsConj a b` is defined as the existence of a unit `c` with `c * a * c⁻¹ = b`.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes

anchor.snippet: This is an equivalence relation whose equivalence classes are called conjugacy classes

annotations.note: `ConjClasses α` is the quotient of the monoid by the `IsConj` setoid, i.e. the set of conjugacy classes.

annotations.label: Class functions

anchor.snippet: Functions that are constant for members of the same conjugacy class are called class functions

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general `ClassFunction` type, but `FDRep.char_conj` shows characters are class functions and any function on `ConjClasses` is one.

annotations.label: Conjugate subsets

anchor.snippet: may be extended from elements to subsets

annotations.note: `conjugatesOfSet` collects all conjugates of elements of a set; the conjugate `gSg⁻¹` of a subgroup is given by the pointwise action (`MulAut.conj g • H`), but no dedicated subset-conjugacy relation is defined.

annotations.label: Normal subgroup characterization

anchor.snippet: A normal subgroup is defined by the property that its conjugacy class contains a single member, namely itself

annotations.note: `Subgroup.Normal` is defined by conjugation-closure, equivalent (via `Normal.conj_smul_eq_self`) to the conjugate subgroup equaling itself.

annotations.label: Inner automorphism

anchor.snippet: Such an automorphism is called an inner automorphism

annotations.note: `MulAut.conj : G →* MulAut G` sends `g` to the inner automorphism `h ↦ g * h * g⁻¹`.

annotations.label: Matrix similarity as conjugacy

anchor.snippet: which is the same condition as matrix similarity

annotations.note: Matrix similarity is exactly `IsConj` on the matrix monoid (units = invertible matrices), but is not separately named in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sylow subgroups are conjugate

anchor.snippet: are conjugates of each other

annotations.note: This instance (Sylow's second theorem) states `G` acts pretransitively on Sylow `p`-subgroups, i.e. they are all conjugate.

annotations.label: Conjugate elements (formal)

anchor.snippet: are conjugate if there exists an element

annotations.note: `isConj_iff` gives `IsConj a b ↔ ∃ c, c * a * c⁻¹ = b` in a group, the textbook definition.

annotations.label: Matrix similarity

anchor.snippet: the conjugacy relation is called matrix similarity

annotations.note: Matrix similarity is `IsConj` specialized to the matrix monoid, with no dedicated `Matrix.similar` definition.

annotations.label: Conjugacy is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: conjugacy is an equivalence relation and therefore partitions

annotations.note: `IsConj.setoid` packages `IsConj.refl`, `IsConj.symm`, `IsConj.trans` into an equivalence relation.

anchors.snippet: conjugacy is an equivalence relation and therefore partitions

annotations.label: Class number

anchor.snippet: is the number of distinct (nonequivalent) conjugacy classes

annotations.note: The class number is expressible as `Nat.card (ConjClasses G)` (used e.g. in `commProb_def'`), but no standalone class-number constant exists.

anchors.snippet: is the number of distinct (nonequivalent) conjugacy classes

annotations.label: Center and singleton classes

anchor.snippet: if and only if its conjugacy class has only one element

annotations.note: `ConjClasses.mk_bijOn` shows the center bijects with the singleton (non-`noncenter`) conjugacy classes; `IsConj.eq_of_left_mem_center` gives the elementwise direction.

annotations.label: Conjugate elements share order

anchor.snippet: then they have the same order

annotations.note: `SemiconjBy.orderOf_eq` gives equal orders for semiconjugate (hence conjugate) elements.

annotations.label: Powers of conjugates are conjugate

anchor.snippet: then so are their powers

annotations.note: `IsConj.pow` states `IsConj a b → IsConj (a ^ n) (b ^ n)`.

annotations.label: Quotient by center is inner automorphism group

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to the group of inner automorphisms of

annotations.note: `MulAut.conj` (whose kernel is the center and whose range is the inner automorphisms) exists, but the isomorphism `G ⧸ Z(G) ≃* Inn(G)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes of S3

anchor.snippet: has three conjugacy classes

annotations.note: Conjugacy of permutations is classified by cycle type, but the specific count of 3 classes for S₃ is not stated.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes of S4

anchor.snippet: has five conjugacy classes

annotations.note: Cycle-type classification of conjugacy classes exists, but the specific count of 5 classes for S₄ is not stated.

annotations.label: Number of classes in symmetric group

anchor.snippet: is equal to the number of integer partitions of

annotations.note: `partition_eq_of_isConj` shows conjugacy classes correspond to partitions via `Equiv.Perm.partition`, but the counting bijection `ConjClasses (Perm) ≃ Nat.Partition n` is not stated.

anchors.snippet: is equal to the number of integer partitions of

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes of dihedral group

anchor.snippet: has four conjugacy classes

annotations.note: `card_conjClasses_odd` counts conjugacy classes of `DihedralGroup n` only for odd `n`; the even case in the article is not covered.

annotations.label: Conjugation group action; orbits are classes

anchor.section: Conjugation as a group action, centralizers, and the class equation
anchor.snippet: The orbits of this action are the conjugacy classes

annotations.note: `ConjAct G` acts by conjugation and `orbit_eq_carrier_conjClasses`/`orbitRel_conjAct` identify its orbits with conjugacy classes.

anchors.section: Conjugation as a group action, centralizers, and the class equation
anchors.snippet: The orbits of this action are the conjugacy classes

annotations.label: Class corresponds to centralizer cosets

anchor.snippet: are in one-to-one correspondence with cosets of the centralizer

annotations.note: Orbit ≃ G/stabilizer combined with `ConjAct.stabilizer_eq_centralizer` gives the bijection between a conjugacy class and cosets of the centralizer.

anchors.snippet: are in one-to-one correspondence with cosets of the centralizer

annotations.label: Class size divides group order

anchor.snippet: the size of each conjugacy class divides the order of the group

annotations.note: `ConjClasses.card_carrier` gives class size = `card G / card (centralizer)`, hence (via the orbit–stabilizer product) it divides the group order.

annotations.label: Class equation

anchor.snippet: gives rise to the class equation

annotations.note: This is the class equation: `card (center G) + Σ noncentral class sizes = card G`.

anchors.snippet: gives rise to the class equation

annotations.label: Finite p-group has nontrivial center

anchor.snippet: has a center of size greater than 1

annotations.note: `IsPGroup.center_nontrivial` states a nontrivial finite p-group has nontrivial center.

annotations.label: Groups of order p^2 are abelian

anchor.snippet: we can further show that

annotations.note: `commutative_of_card_eq_prime_sq` (and `commGroupOfCardEqPrimeSq`) prove groups of order `p²` are commutative.

annotations.label: Burnside average centralizer

anchor.snippet: the number of conjugacy classes of a finite group

annotations.note: This states `#{commuting pairs} = #ConjClasses × |G|`, equivalent to the number of classes being the average centralizer size.

annotations.label: Conjugate subsets

anchor.section: Conjugacy of subgroups and general subsets
anchor.snippet: if there exists some

annotations.note: Conjugation of a subgroup as `gHg⁻¹` is realized by the pointwise action `MulAut.conj g • H`, but the binary subset-conjugacy relation is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Subset class corresponds to normalizer cosets

anchor.section: Conjugacy of subgroups and general subsets
anchor.snippet: one-to-one correspondence between elements of the conjugacy class of

annotations.note: Only for Sylow subgroups is stabilizer = normalizer proved (with orbit ≃ G/normalizer); the general subset/subgroup statement is not formalized.

anchors.section: Conjugacy of subgroups and general subsets
anchors.snippet: one-to-one correspondence between elements of the conjugacy class of

annotations.label: Conjugate subgroups are isomorphic

anchor.section: Conjugacy of subgroups and general subsets
anchor.snippet: Conjugate subgroups are isomorphic

annotations.note: `Subgroup.equivSMul` gives `H ≃* (a • H)`, the isomorphism between a subgroup and its conjugate.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes as free homotopy classes

anchor.snippet: can be thought of as equivalence classes of free loops under free homotopy

annotations.note: No formalization relating conjugacy classes of the fundamental group to free homotopy classes of loops was found.

annotations.label: Irreducible representations equal conjugacy classes

anchor.section: Conjugacy class and irreducible representations in finite group
anchor.snippet: is precisely the number of conjugacy classes

annotations.note: Character orthogonality for irreducible representations is formalized, but the theorem equating the number of irreducibles with the number of conjugacy classes is not.
